title: Water Act Revisions Called for by Recovery and Resilience Plan, Get Parliament's Approval on First Reading
description: Parliament approved on first reading a bill amending the Water Act, submitted by Georgi Chakurov and a group of MPs. The proposed amendments were backed by 120 lawmakers from Progressive Bulgaria and the Movement for Rights and Freedoms (MRF), while 54 MPs from GERB-UDF, Democratic Bulgaria, We Continue the Change, and Vazrazhdane abstained. The revisions are called for by requirements set out in Bulgaria’s National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP). They aim to guarantee minimum costs for the use of water supply and sewerage systems and services, as well as create conditions for consolidation in the management and operation of water and sewerage infrastructure and the provision of related services. The bill also includes proposed amendments and supplements to the Water Supply and Sewerage Services Regulation Act needed to achieve these objectives. The draft legislation introduces unified nationwide indicators for the quality and efficiency of water and sewerage services. It also envisages differentiated pricing for household and non-household consumers, as well as for social infrastructure facilities, in order to ensure socially affordable prices while maintaining the ability to properly maintain, modernize, and develop water infrastructure systems. In addition, the bill stipulates that water and sewerage services within designated territories should be provided by a single operator. The deadline for submitting proposals between the first and second readings of the amendments was shortened to three days.

title: Historical achievement pushing the country to the absolute bottom in every field
description: Whilst the country hits rock bottom in global rankings, Erdoğan’s “historical achievement” benefits capital rather than the public. During 23 years of governance, capital has grown through public resources, whereas the country ranks among the worst in the world regarding inflation, income inequality, labour, and corruption.

title: In today's digital age, paper ephemera attracts new generation of collectors
description: We have less need for paper than past generations, but paper ephemera is still very collectible. We meet some digital natives who collect old postcards and comics at a paper show in Pennsylvania.

title: The ‘big question’ Matthew Lloyd has asked amid big week for ‘broken’ Essendon
description: Essendon great Matthew Lloyd has addressed the big week at Essendon, with multiple departures including head coach Brad Scott. Lloyd thought it was the right call to move on Scott, and he’s addressed the talk around his former teammate James Hird, who is keen to return to the top job. And Lloyd has asked a [...]
source: 3aw - Melbourne's Favourite News And Talk Station

title: Wings soar past Dragons in VBA upset
description: Ho Chi Minh City Wings showed off a different face in the new season when beating Danang Dragons 95-82 in the Vietnam Professional Basketball League (VBA) 2026's game 5 on May 28 in Đà Nẵng City.

title: Justice Department opens investigation into Trump's accuser E. Jean Carroll
description: The Justice Department takes aim at another perceived enemy of President Trump, opening an investigation into E. Jean Carroll, who won two lawsuits against the president.
